question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the permanent caching of openApiMap (never invalidated) to cause a freeze-related default value injected during startup to persist for the entire server lifetime, permanently affecting all token freeze-status queries even after the underlying spec is corrected?
